scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a normal departure only after manifest, guide, transport, ticket, pickup, and accessibility-note evidence aligned.
Missing ticket allotment and supplier confirmation produced a held state and evidence request instead of skipped validation.
Refund and private-vehicle demands were treated as policy-exception work outside tour operations authority.
Booking, manifest, ticket, payment, and duplicate-voucher conflicts stopped completion and routed reconciliation to the right owners.
Guide substitution respected credential and permit constraints and escalated when compliant rework was unavailable.
Unverified passenger-carrier status triggered transport or safety escalation instead of unsupported safety clearance.
Suspicious ticket transfer and refund reroute signals triggered fraud escalation rather than unauthorized changes.
High-volume weather disruption was segmented by departure, guest impact, readiness evidence, and owner authority.
Completion was refused until guide closeout, ticket reconciliation, no-show, transport, and exception disposition evidence existed.
Tour-specific terminology and artifacts were preserved instead of reverting to generic operations language.
Conflicting venue, tenant, and public fee-disclosure policies were recorded and escalated for precedence resolution.
Tenant-specific pickup, credential, refund-threshold, and emergency rules were applied without losing the public safety and consumer baseline.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
The package stays inside tour operations and does not claim booking, pricing, refund, supplier-substitution, fraud, safety, legal, or permit approval authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
